title: Nematanthus tessmannii
intro: Nematanthus tessmannii, also known as Tessmann's whorled begonia or Brazilian begonia, is a plant species in the family Gesneriaceae, characterized by its glossy leaves and vibrant flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Nematanthus tessmannii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas and infusions, as well as in traditional remedies such as salves and tinctures.
taxonomy: Nematanthus tessmannii is classified within the plant family Gesneriaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the "African violet family". This family comprises over 3,000 species, many of which are epiphytes or terrestrial plants with showy flowers. Nematanthus tessmannii is a genus of flowering plants native to South America, and it is part of the Gesneriaceae family. The species is further classified into the subfamily Gesnerioideae, which includes many plant species with similar characteristics. Nematanthus tessmannii is a specific species within the Nematanthus genus.
